input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan:

## .NET MAUI Dev Environment — Windows 11 (Android + Windows)

### 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

### 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
dotnet workload install maui
# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

### 3. Install Visual Studio 2022 (recommended) or Build Tools

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
```

Then add required components via the VS Installer, or script it:

```powershell
# Modify an existing install with the needed workload IDs
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installershell.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --includeRecommended --quiet --wait
```

### 4. Install Android SDK (if not bundled via VS)

```powershell
# Option A — Use the SDK that ships with VS:
#   Located at: $env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk
#
# Option B — Install standalone via commandlinetools:
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidHome, "User")

# Download command-line tools
Invoke-WebRequest `
  -Uri "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip" `
  -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp"

# Google expects a specific directory structure
New-Item -Path "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest" -ItemType Directory -Force
Move-Item "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp\cmdline-tools\*" "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\"
Remove-Item "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp" -Recurse
```

### 5. Discover & Install Correct Android Packages

```powershell
$sdkmanager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List all available packages to find the right versions dynamically
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Sort-Object
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;"    | Sort-Object
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "system-images;"  | Sort-Object

# Then install (update versions based on the list output above)
& $sdkmanager --install `
  "platform-tools" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses   # answer 'y' to all prompts
```

### 6. (Optional) Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdmanager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

& $avdmanager create avd `
  --name "Pixel_MAUI" `
  --package "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_7"

# Launch it
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

### 7. Enable Windows App SDK Target (for WinUI 3)

```powershell
# Developer mode is required for sideloading
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"   # toggle Developer Mode ON

# The Windows App SDK comes in via the maui workload — verify:
dotnet workload list   # should show "maui-windows"
```

### 8. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# --- Build for Windows ---
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# --- Build for Android ---
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# --- Run on Windows ---
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# --- Run on Android emulator ---
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

### 9. Discover What TFMs Are Available

If you're unsure about the exact target framework moniker:

```powershell
# See what frameworks the template generated:
Select-String -Path *.csproj -Pattern "TargetFrameworks"

# Check installed Android API levels:
Get-ChildItem "$env:ANDROID_HOME\platforms"

# Check installed Windows SDK versions:
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include"
```

### Quick Troubleshooting

| Problem | Fix |
|---|---|
| `JAVA_HOME` not set | VS bundles JDK at `C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-*`. Set `JAVA_HOME` to that path. |
| Emulator won't start | Enable **Hyper-V** and **WHPX**: `Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All` |
| Workload restore fails | Run `dotnet workload repair` |

This covers end-to-end setup. Adjust the API level numbers (`android-35`, `net9.0`, etc.) based on the `--list` output when you run the commands.
